notes: Ground-truth fixtures for ast-test-analysis authoritative observations and IMPLEMENTATION_ASSERTION detection. Files reference real source paths. Covers 4 categories: clean spec (zero authoritative), MOCK_INTERNAL + MISSING_CLEANUP only, IMPLEMENTATION_ASSERTION hook-call-args, and mixed (all authoritative types). Each fixture documents every authoritative observation the tool currently emits.

expected.notes: Clean spec. Uses vi.mock for external boundaries only (services, providers). Assertions are render-based (screen.getByText, screen.getByRole). Zero authoritative observations. This is what a compliant container test looks like.

observations.notes: Mocks an internal module (likely a sibling component or local utility). High confidence because the mock target is within the same feature directory.
Second internal mock. Medium confidence -- may be a shared utility that is closer to a boundary.

No afterEach cleanup detected. Global vi.restoreAllMocks() in vitest.setup.ts handles this, but the tool does not trace global setup files -- it only checks within the spec file. This is a known false positive pattern for the MISSING_CLEANUP observation.

expected.notes: Has MOCK_INTERNAL and MISSING_CLEANUP authoritative observations but zero IMPLEMENTATION_ASSERTION. The spec mocks internal modules but asserts on rendered output, not hook call args.

observations.notes: Mocks an internal module within the operational-status feature.
Asserts useTeamRealtimeStatsQuery was called with specific args. This tests hook wiring (implementation detail), not user-visible behavior.
Second hook call assertion with different filter args.
Third hook call assertion. All three test that the query hook receives the correct filters -- a valid concern but testing at the wrong layer.

expected.notes: Mixed authoritative observations: 1 MOCK_INTERNAL + 3 IMPLEMENTATION_ASSERTION. The spec tests query hook arg forwarding instead of asserting on rendered data. Classic P1/P8 violation.

observations.notes: Asserts mutation hook was called with specific config. Tests hook wiring, not that the form submission produced visible feedback.
Asserts delete mutation hook call args.
Asserts update mutation hook call args.
No local afterEach cleanup. Same global-setup false positive pattern as ChatContainer.

expected.notes: Heavy authoritative load: 2 MOCK_INTERNAL + 3 IMPLEMENTATION_ASSERTION (mutation hooks) + 1 MISSING_CLEANUP. All three CRUD mutations are tested by asserting on hook call args instead of user-visible outcomes (toast, table row change, dialog close).
